mentions.note: Trades below 12x next year's earnings despite an ~80% return last year; improving Medicare Advantage rates and stabilizing retail pharmacy support further re-rating.
Better than UnitedHealth with Aetna integrated; David Joyner has cleaned up problems; higher yield makes it preferred healthcare pick.
Better than UNH; higher yield; includes Aetna; David Joyner cleaned up problems; preferred healthcare play over UNH.
Health insurance business tumbled 14% on Medicare Advantage rate freeze; insurance division facing structural margin pressure.
CEO David Joiner performing admirably; now healthcare's only national drugstore chain as Walgreens goes private—fascinating entry.
Recommended alongside Ulta as quality healthcare/beauty retailer; preferable to speculative beauty plays.
CEO David Joyner is real deal creating national drugstore chain; holding through weakness; recommend continuing to own.

Trades 11x earnings with terrific quarter from CEO David Joyner; owns 8,932 drugstores; only two competitors left (Rite Aid bankrupt, Walgreens private); upside from disappearing competition; preferred to ServiceNow.
Pressing bet on good quarter; CEO David Joiner fixing company while Rite Aid closed and Walgreens self-immolates. Aetna buried within likely solid too.

Q1 magnificent quarter: 39¢ EPS beat $2.18 basis, higher revenue, FY guidance raised; stock +7.7%, 3-yr high; Joyner turnaround real—five quarters Aetna improvement, pricing discipline, Health100 app launching.
Outsized gains in health insurers and drug distributors; Cramer expects CVS gains to be long-lasting as private credit panic fades and earnings improve.
CEO David Joyner navigated competitive scrum; Walgreens largely gone, Amazon lacks insurance arm; CVS-Aetna synergy crushing it; strong buy.
